title: gochnatia maisiana var. parviflora
intro: Gochnatia maisiana var. parviflora, also known as Mexican teabush, is a shrub-like plant native to Mexico and Central America, belonging to the Asteraceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its rich content of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and terpenoids. The health benefits of Gochnatia maisiana var. parviflora include its potential to alleviate pain, reduce fever, and support the immune system, as well as its use as an antiseptic to treat wounds and infections. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its leaves can also be dried and used as a herbal supplement.
